What is football?: Football is a sport played between two teams of eleven players attempting to score goals.
How many players are on a football team?: A football team has eleven players on the field.
How long is a football match?: A standard football match lasts ninety minutes plus added time.
What is a goal in football?: A goal is scored when the ball completely crosses the goal line between the posts.
What is offside in football?: Offside is a rule that prevents players from gaining an unfair positional advantage.
What is a penalty kick?: A penalty kick is awarded after certain fouls inside the penalty area.
What is a free kick?: A free kick is awarded after a foul is committed.
What is a corner kick?: A corner kick is awarded when the defending team last touches the ball before it crosses the goal line.
What is a throw-in?: A throw-in restarts play when the ball leaves the field over the touchline.
What is added time?: Added time compensates for stoppages during the match.
What is VAR?: VAR stands for Video Assistant Referee.
What is FIFA?: FIFA is the international governing body of football.
What is UEFA?: UEFA is the governing body of European football.
What is the FIFA World Cup?: The FIFA World Cup is the most prestigious international football tournament.
How often is the FIFA World Cup held?: The FIFA World Cup is held every four years.
What is the UEFA Champions League?: The UEFA Champions League is Europe's top club football competition.
What is the Premier League?: The Premier League is the top football division in England.
What is La Liga?: La Liga is the top football division in Spain.
What is Serie A?: Serie A is the top football division in Italy.
What is the Bundesliga?: The Bundesliga is the top football division in Germany.
What is Ligue 1?: Ligue 1 is the top football division in France.

What is the UEFA European Championship?: The UEFA European Championship is the main national team tournament in Europe.
What is a hat-trick?: A hat-trick occurs when a player scores three goals in one match.
What is a clean sheet?: A clean sheet means a team conceded no goals in a match.
What is a striker?: A striker is a player whose primary role is scoring goals.
What is a midfielder?: A midfielder plays between defense and attack.
What is a defender?: A defender helps prevent the opposing team from scoring.
What is a goalkeeper?: A goalkeeper protects the goal and can use their hands inside the penalty area.

What is a red card?: A red card sends a player off the field.
What is extra time?: Extra time consists of two additional fifteen-minute periods.
What is a penalty shootout?: A penalty shootout decides a match when teams remain tied after extra time.
What is a derby?: A derby is a match between rival teams from the same area.

What is the Paralympics?: The Paralympics are international games for athletes with disabilities.
What is swimming?: Swimming is a sport involving racing in water.
Who is Michael Phelps?: Michael Phelps is the most decorated Olympian in history.
What is a relay race?: A relay race involves team members taking turns completing parts of a race.
What is athletics?: Athletics includes running, jumping, and throwing events.
Who is Usain Bolt?: Usain Bolt is widely considered the fastest man in history.
What is a marathon?: A marathon is a long-distance running race of 42.195 kilometers.
What is cycling?: Cycling is a sport involving bicycle racing.
What is the Tour de France?: The Tour de France is the most famous cycling race in the world.
What is rugby?: Rugby is a team sport played with an oval ball.
What is rugby union?: Rugby union is the most widely played form of rugby.
What is rugby league?: Rugby league is a variation of rugby with different rules.
What is cricket?: Cricket is a bat-and-ball sport popular in many countries.
What is a century in cricket?: A century is when a player scores one hundred runs in a single innings.
What is field hockey?: Field hockey is a team sport played with sticks and a ball.
What is ice hockey?: Ice hockey is a fast-paced sport played on ice.
What is the NHL?: The NHL is the top professional ice hockey league in North America.
What is esports?: Esports are organized competitive video gaming events.
What is League of Legends esports?: League of Legends esports is one of the largest competitive gaming scenes.
What is CS esports?: Counter-Strike esports is one of the most popular competitive shooter scenes.
What is the Super Bowl?: The Super Bowl is the championship game of the NFL.
What is American football?: American football is a team sport played with an oval ball.
What is the NFL?: The NFL is the top professional American football league.
Who is Tom Brady?: Tom Brady is considered one of the greatest American football players ever.
What is a touchdown?: A touchdown scores six points in American football.
What is badminton?: Badminton is a racket sport played with a shuttlecock.
What is table tennis?: Table tennis is a sport played on a table using paddles and a lightweight ball.
What is chess boxing?: Chess boxing alternates rounds of chess and boxing.
What is handball?: Handball is a team sport where players throw a ball into a goal.
What is fencing?: Fencing is a combat sport involving swords.
What is judo?: Judo is a Japanese martial art focused on throws and grappling.
What is karate?: Karate is a martial art originating from Japan.
What is taekwondo?: Taekwondo is a Korean martial art known for kicking techniques.
What is surfing?: Surfing is a sport where athletes ride ocean waves.
What is skateboarding?: Skateboarding is a sport performed on a skateboard.
What is snowboarding?: Snowboarding involves descending snowy slopes on a board.
What is climbing?: Sport climbing involves ascending artificial or natural rock surfaces.
What is rowing?: Rowing is a sport where athletes propel boats using oars.
What is archery?: Archery is the sport of shooting arrows at targets.
What is weightlifting?: Weightlifting is a sport focused on lifting heavy weights overhead.
What is powerlifting?: Powerlifting is a strength sport involving squat, bench press, and deadlift.
What is bodybuilding?: Bodybuilding focuses on muscle development and physique competition.
Who was Alan Turing?: Alan Turing (1912–1954) was a British mathematician, computer scientist, and cryptanalyst, widely considered the father of computing and artificial intelligence.
What does AI stand for?: AI stands for Artificial Intelligence, the simulation of human intelligence processes by machines
What does ML stand for?: ML stands for Machine Learning, a subset of AI focused on building systems that learn from data
What is the purpose of Git?: Git is a version control system used to track changes in source code during software development
What is a frontend developer?: A frontend developer builds the visual and interactive elements of a website or application that users see and interact with
What is a backend developer?: A backend developer builds and maintains the server-side logic, databases, and APIs that power the application behind the scenes
What is a full stack developer?: A full stack developer is a programmer who can work on both the frontend and backend parts of an application
What does SaaS stand for?: SaaS stands for Software as a Service, a method of delivering applications over the internet as a subscription
What is the difference between Java and: Java is an object-oriented programming language used for backend apps and systems, while JavaScript is primarily a scripting language used to make web pages interactive
What is a responsive web design?: Responsive web design is an approach to web development that makes web pages render well on a variety of devices and window or screen sizes
What is malware?: Malware is malicious software designed to disrupt, damage, or gain unauthorized access to a computer system
What is phishing?: Phishing is a type of social engineering attack where criminals trick users into revealing sensitive data by pretending to be a trustworthy entity
What is a firewall?: A firewall is a network security device that monitors and filters incoming and outgoing network traffic based on an organization's previously established security policies
What does VPN stand for?: VPN stands for Virtual Private Network, which encrypts your internet traffic and hides your online identity
What is a cookie in web browsing?: A cookie is a small piece of data stored on the user's computer by the web browser while browsing a website, used to remember state information or track activity
What is an open-source project?: An open-source project is software whose source code is made available to the public for anyone to view, modify, and distribute
What is cloud storage?: Cloud storage is a model of computer data storage where the digital data is stored in logical pools across servers managed by a hosting company
What is IoT?: IoT, or Internet of Things, refers to the network of physical objects embedded with sensors and software for the purpose of connecting and exchanging data over the internet
What is blockchain?: Blockchain is a decentralized, distributed ledger technology that securely records transactions across a network of computers
What is data mining?: Data mining is the process of uncovering patterns and other valuable information from large data sets
What is a bug in computer programming?: A bug is an error, flaw, or fault in a design, development, or operation of computer software that causes it to produce an incorrect or unexpected result
What does IP stand for in networking?: IP stands for Internet Protocol, which is the set of rules governing the format of data sent over the internet or local network
What does DNS do?: The DNS, or Domain Name System, acts as the phonebook of the internet by translating human-readable domain names like google.com into numerical IP addresses
What is the purpose of a database?: A database is used to store, organize, manage, and retrieve electronic data efficiently
What is SQL?: SQL, or Structured Query Language, is the standard programming language used to manage and manipulate relational databases
What is NoSQL?: NoSQL is an approach to database management that design databases to accommodate non-relational data structures like documents or graphs
What is Python used for?: Python is a versatile programming language widely used for web development, automation, data science, artificial intelligence, and software scripting
What is a framework in programming?: A framework is a pre-built platform of libraries and tools that provides a foundation for developers to build applications more quickly and consistently
What is Docker?: Docker is an open-source platform used to automate the deployment of applications inside lightweight, portable containers
What is the main function of an Operatin: An operating system manages a computer's hardware resources and provides common services for computer programs
What is the difference between SSD and H: An HDD is a traditional storage device with mechanical spinning platters, while an SSD uses flash memory to read and write data much faster and without moving parts
What is a CPU clock speed?: Clock speed measures the number of cycles your CPU executes per second, usually measured in gigahertz
What is object-oriented programming?: Object-oriented programming, or OOP, is a programming paradigm based on the concept of objects, which can contain data and code
What is the function of a router?: A router is a networking device that forwards data packets between computer networks, directing internet traffic to its destination
What does UI stand for?: UI stands for User Interface, which is the series of screens, pages, and visual elements like buttons and icons that enable a person to interact with a product or service
What does UX stand for?: UX stands for User Experience, which focuses on the overall experience and satisfaction a user has when interacting with a digital product
What is a bit?: A bit is the smallest unit of data in a computer, representing a logical state with one of two values: 0 or 1
How many bits are in a byte?: There are exactly 8 bits in a single byte
What is Big Data?: Big Data refers to data sets that are too large or complex to be dealt with by traditional data-processing application software
What is an absolute path in file systems: An absolute path is a file path that specifies the complete location of a file or directory from the root element of the file system
What is a relative path in file systems?: A relative path is a file path that specifies the location of a file or directory relative to the current working directory
What is automated testing?: Automated testing is the practice of executing software tests automatically using scripted code to verify that the application works as expected
What does CI/CD stand for?: CI/CD stands for Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment, a set of practices that automates the integration, testing, and deployment of code changes
